channel.title: Twitter Timeline RSS
channel.description: 你的 Twitter Following Timeline（via opencli）

item.title: In jazz, there's no script: You have to figure things out as you go along. Sometimes you need to sit...
item.description:
In jazz, there's no script: You have to figure things out as you go along. Sometimes you need to sit back and let others drive things; other times, you blare it out yourself. To do the right thing at the right moment you need to really listen to the people you're playing with so that you can understand where they're going.
All great creative collaboration should feel the same way. Combining your different skills like different instruments, improvising creatively, and at the same time subordinating yourself to the goals of the group leads to playing great music together. But it's important to keep in mind what number of collaborators will play well together: A talented duo can improvise beautifully, as can a trio or quartet. But gather ten musicians and no matter how talented they are, it's probably going to be too many unless they're carefully orchestrated. #principleoftheday
--- @RayDalio | ❤️ 9 | 🔁 1 | 💬 1 | 👁 1337
item.author: @RayDalio

item.title: https://t.co/aWbr5iZWbp

item.title: Wishful thinking is a multi trillion dollar market.
item.author: @tunguz

item.title:
My takeaway: AI in HR is a context problem disguised as a tooling problem.
You must teach the syst...
item.description:
My takeaway: AI in HR is a context problem disguised as a tooling problem.
You must teach the system how your organization thinks.
Where is AI breaking down in your HR workflow?
Comment “HR” &amp; I’ll share how to use AI in HR effectively.
#CorbeyPartner
--- @Ronald_vanLoon | ❤️ 0 | 🔁 0 | 💬 0 | 👁 58
item.author: @Ronald_vanLoon

item.title:
The insight that stood out:
HR decisions depend on layered context.
Not just general rules, but pr...
item.description:
The insight that stood out:
HR decisions depend on layered context.
Not just general rules, but priorities.
For example:
→ National legislation sets the baseline → Company policy often overrides in practice → Edge cases change everything
If your AI is not trained on this, it will give the wrong answers confidently.
--- @Ronald_vanLoon | ❤️ 0 | 🔁 0 | 💬 1 | 👁 65
item.author: @Ronald_vanLoon

item.title:
Most HR AI projects don’t fail because the tech is weak.
They fail because the AI has no idea how y...
item.description:
Most HR AI projects don’t fail because the tech is weak.
They fail because the AI has no idea how your company actually works.
That is the missing link.
I spoke with Anna Fedosova, co-founder of Corbey, about why this keeps happening.
Here’s the breakdown... https://t.co/v9yVZzfV3P
--- @Ronald_vanLoon | ❤️ 1 | 🔁 0 | 💬 2 | 👁 101
item.author: @Ronald_vanLoon

item.title: No amount of fast thinking will ever add up to slow thinking.

item.title: OpenAI vs. Anthropic gets all the attention, but @GoogleLabs is quietly shipping some of the most in...
item.description:
OpenAI vs. Anthropic gets all the attention, but @GoogleLabs is quietly shipping some of the most interesting AI products right now.
I made a 16-min video showing you what 5 Labs products are capable of:
1. Pomelli for marketing 2. Stitch for design 3. Genie for 3D worlds 4. Flow for videos 5. NotebookLM for synthesis
📌 Watch now: https://t.co/VztXVKO5Gu
--- @petergyang | ❤️ 44 | 🔁 4 | 💬 9 | 👁 2462
item.author: @petergyang
